user_input:
Dear IT team
I am Alejo Nevado-Holgado, user "n_neva1" of Dawn. A couple of months ago I was trying to SSH into dawn from my laptop, but in order to do that I first had to create an SSH key with the following command:
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -a 100 -C "dn_neva1@dawn (AIRR-p19)"
Then I sent the public key to this email address in a file called "id_ed25519.pub". A couple of days ago I reinstalled Ubuntu, and now I am not able to SSH into Dawn again. I didn't save the private side of the key that I generated before. I was wondering, how could I connect to Dawn again from my laptop? Claude says that I have to generate a new key and send to you the new "id_ed25519.pub" file. I attach it in case this would actually work.
Very best and many thanks for your help
Alejo
Alejo Nevado-Holgado
Associate Professor
Department of Psychiatry & Big Data Institute | University of Oxford | Oxford | OX3 7JX
alejo.nevado-holgado@psych.ox.ac.uk<[alejo.nevado-holgado@psych.ox.ac.uk](mailto:alejo.nevado-holgado@psych.ox.ac.uk)>
[cid:108711ff-f78d-4b60-b2a2-bb8b3941fc7a]  [cid:af762036-bceb-4b4b-a09e-6924cd209afa]
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH Keys (Dawn)
Technical Action Required: Yes (append public keys)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User lost the private key after reinstall and needs a replacement key registered. Each client device requires its own valid key pair, and the corresponding public key must be appended server-side.

ACTION
1) Append new public key for replacement laptop key.
2) Clarify per-device key model (one key pair per device).
3) If attachments fail, request public key pasted as plain text in email body.
4) If the user later needs access from another device, repeat the same process for that device's public key.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Alejo,

You will need a new valid SSH key pair on the laptop you reinstalled. Please send the replacement public key for that device (you can paste it directly as plain text in the email body), and we can append it to your account.

If you also connect from other devices, each device will need its own key pair and public-key registration.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Never request private key material.
- Accept only public keys.
- Confirm key addition only after staff-side update is complete.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SSH Access / Key Management [1][2]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user, Alejo Nevado-Holgado, is experiencing issues with SSH access to Dawn after reinstalling Ubuntu and losing the private key [1]. The user has generated a new key pair and is requesting assistance with adding the public key to their account [1][2].

ACTION
To resolve the issue, the new public key needs to be added to the user's account [1][2]. The user has already generated a new key pair and attached the public key to the email [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Verify the user's identity and ensure the new public key is correctly formatted [1][2].
2. Add the new public key to the user's account [1][2].
3. Inform the user to try connecting to Dawn via SSH again and report any further issues [1][2].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Alejo,
I have added your new public key to your account. Please try connecting to Dawn via SSH again and let me know if you encounter any further issues.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the user's identity is verified before adding a new public key to their account [1][2].
- Only add public keys to user accounts, never private keys [1][2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-75228
[2] : HPCSSUP-97179

user_input:
To whom it may concern,
We have a job on HPC that takes more than 36 hours to run, and we would like to try the check point method. Could you provide some examples of how to use the check point?
Thanks,
Qi
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Runtime / Checkpointing Guidance
Technical Action Required: No (advisory)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User asks for checkpointing examples for jobs exceeding wall time. Provide concise, generic checkpoint patterns (periodic save + resume path), point to the linked reference, and clarify that implementation must be adapted to the user’s application and CSD3 environment.

ACTION
1) Explain user-level checkpointing concept.
2) Provide 1-2 generic checkpoint patterns the user can apply.
3) Provide external reference as conceptual guidance.
4) Clarify that commands/examples may need adaptation for local environment.

EXAMPLE CHECKPOINT PATTERNS (GENERIC)
1) Periodic state save + restart
   - Run application so it writes restart files every N minutes/iterations to a checkpoint directory.
   - On next job run, detect latest checkpoint and resume from it instead of starting from scratch.

2) Pre-timeout save + requeue/next run
   - Configure application (or wrapper) to write a final checkpoint before wall-time expiry.
   - Submit the next job to resume from that checkpoint file.

Generic wrapper pattern:
```
if [ -f checkpoints/latest.ckpt ]; then
  my_app --resume checkpoints/latest.ckpt
else
  my_app --start
fi
```

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Qi,

We don’t have a ready CSD3-specific checkpoint script to provide, but the standard approach is:

1) write restart/checkpoint files periodically during the run, and  
2) start the next job by resuming from the latest checkpoint.

Two generic patterns you can use are:
- Periodic checkpointing: save state every N iterations/minutes, then resume from the most recent checkpoint on the next run.
- Pre-timeout checkpointing: write a final checkpoint before wall-time is reached, then submit the next job to resume from it.

For example, your wrapper logic can be:

```
if [ -f checkpoints/latest.ckpt ]; then
  my_app --resume checkpoints/latest.ckpt
else
  my_app --start
fi
```

This overview may help:
https://rc-docs.northeastern.edu/en/latest/best-practices/checkpointing.html#the-checkpointing-technique

Please note these are generic patterns and may need adaptation for your application and CSD3.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply native automatic checkpoint support unless confirmed.

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is looking to use checkpointing for a job that takes more than 36 hours to run on the HPC [1]. Checkpointing is a method to save the state of a job at regular intervals, allowing it to be restarted from the last checkpoint in case of an interruption [2]. However, the user is unsure of how to implement checkpointing.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To use checkpointing, the user can utilize the DMTCP (Distributed Multi-Threaded Checkpointing) tool [5]. However, it is noted that DMTCP is no longer supported [3]. An alternative approach is to manually save the state of the job at regular intervals, allowing it to be restarted from the last saved state [2]. The user can also consider splitting the job into smaller chunks or using a different queue with a longer time limit [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about the job you are trying to run, such as the code and input data?
- Have you considered using a different queue or partition, such as the long queue, for your job?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To use checkpointing for your job, you can try utilizing the DMTCP tool or manually saving the state of your job at regular intervals. If you need further assistance or have questions about implementing checkpointing, please let us know.
Best regards,
HPC Support

- Checkpointing should be used to ensure that long-running jobs can be restarted in case of an interruption, but it may not be suitable for all types of jobs [2].
- The use of DMTCP is no longer supported, and alternative methods should be explored [3].

Hi Peter,
sorry for the delay in getting back to you. Nothing obvious occurs to me as to how we might speed this process up. Furthermore, it is not possible to allow 100 day long jobs. Even if we were to ignore the impact on other users of the system, we would not necessarily expect the system to stay up for 100 continuous days.
However, a potential different solution to this problem would be to use an external checkpoint/restart tool DMTCP. Please see here [1] an example job script that makes use of DMTCP to checkpoint and restart a long running workload.
Best wishes,
Jeffrey.

Dear Support
I started to copy a 108GB zip file to RCS when it occurred to me that this might be a bad idea. It quickly completed anyway. Do you recommend that I don't do this or is it OK?
Kind regards
Simon
reference:

Category: Storage / RCS Transfer Best Practice
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User asks if transferring a 108GB zip file to RCS is problematic. Large archives are acceptable. For RCS workflows, tar is usually preferred; zip is still acceptable where password protection or Windows tooling requires it. Bigger single archives are typically slower to stage/retrieve, so archive layout should match expected access/retrieval patterns.

ACTION
1) Confirm transfer is acceptable.
2) Recommend best-practice docs.
3) Prefer tar where feasible; zip acceptable when required (e.g. password protection).
4) Explain operational trade-off of archive sizing (fewer large files vs faster partial retrieval).
5) Recommend integrity verification after transfer (e.g. checksum comparison).

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Simon,

That is generally fine. For RCS, tar archives are usually preferred, but zip is acceptable if needed (for example for password protection).

The main trade-off is that very large archives can take longer to retrieve from cold storage. If you expect partial restores, splitting data into sensible archive chunks can help operationally.

After transfer, it is also good practice to verify integrity (for example with checksums) between source and destination.

Best-practice guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/best-practice.html

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not present size threshold as a hard prohibition unless policy defines one.
- Do not overstate archive-size rules; frame them as performance/operational trade-offs.

user_input:
We are having problems creating a GPU platform on the “ska-src-science-validations” tenancy on Azimuth (with OpenHPC v3).
I always seem to have the error:
error creating blazar lease - Not enough hosts available
and it doesn’t matter which flavour (standard or v2) to use for login and control nodes.
Does this mean that I’m doing something wrong in the setup, or is there an issue with blazar on that tenancy?
Thanks a lot,
Ed
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Cloud Capacity / Azimuth / Blazar Leasing
Technical Action Required: No (capacity explanation)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`Not enough hosts available` in Blazar indicated resource exhaustion, not a user misconfiguration.

ACTION
1) Explain that message reflects current capacity saturation.
2) Advise retry when capacity frees.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Edward,

That error indicates capacity is currently exhausted in the tenancy; it is not necessarily a setup error on your side.

Please retry when resources become available.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not suggest configuration changes if capacity is the real limiter.

Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The error message "error creating blazar lease - Not enough hosts available" suggests that there are not enough available hosts to create the GPU platform on Azimuth [2]. This issue has been encountered before, and it is likely due to the lack of free Blazar host capacity [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate the current resource allocation and usage in the "ska-src-science-validations" tenancy to determine if there are any available hosts that can be used to create the GPU platform [2].
2. If there are no available hosts, consider terminating or reconfiguring existing platforms to free up resources [1].
3. Alternatively, consider using a different flavor or configuration for the login and control nodes to reduce the resource requirements [2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Are there any existing platforms or resources that can be terminated or reconfigured to free up hosts for the new GPU platform?
- Are there any specific requirements or constraints for the GPU platform that may be contributing to the resource availability issue?

HPC Support

user_input:
Dear HPC support,
I use the university MS authenticator on my phone and I would like to 
set it up as my TOTP for HPC login
Is it possible and how do I do this?
I tried using the TOTP when I attempt to login but it does not seem to 
work.
Thank you!
Cheers, Claudio
– 
Claudio Castelnovo
Professor of Theoretical Physics and Fellow of Trinity College
/Theory of Condensed Matter Group/
Department of Physics, Cavendish Laboratory
University of Cambridge
19, J J Thomson Avenue, Cambridge CB3 0HE, UK
phone: +44 (0)1223 337433
fax: +44 (0)1223 337356
web: [http://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/profiles/cc726/](http://www.tcm.phy.cam.ac.uk/profiles/cc726/)
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account Provisioning / MFA / New User
Technical Action Required: No (process guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User asks whether the Microsoft Authenticator app on their phone can be used for HPC login TOTP and reports that the current code does not work.

The same authenticator app can be used, but CSD3 requires its own separate MFA enrolment/token; a University token will not work for CSD3 SSH or web login. If the user does not yet have an active CSD3 account, account provisioning must be completed before MFA enrolment can work.

ACTION
1) Confirm whether the user already has an active CSD3 account.
2) If not, direct them to the application form:
   https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application
3) Once the account is provisioned, instruct them to enrol CSD3 MFA via the documented process, e.g. SSH to:
   `ssh cc726@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk`
4) Clarify that the same Microsoft Authenticator app can be used, but it must contain a separate CSD3 token.
5) Do NOT:
   - Tell the user to reuse an existing University/UIS token for CSD3.
   - Troubleshoot login-web credentials before account provisioning is confirmed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Claudio,

Yes, you can use the same Microsoft Authenticator app on your phone for HPC login, but CSD3 needs its own separate token. The existing University token will not work for CSD3.

Before troubleshooting the token further, we first need to confirm whether you already have an active CSD3 account. If not, please apply here:

https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/rcs-application

Once your account is active, please follow the MFA setup guide and connect to:

  ssh cc726@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

to enrol the CSD3 MFA token in your authenticator app.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Distinguish account provisioning from MFA setup.
- The app can be reused; the token cannot.
- Do not assume the user can log in via web/SSH before the account exists.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / TOTP Setup
Technical Action Required: Yes
Portal Action Required: No

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to set up Microsoft Authenticator as their TOTP for HPC login, but it's not working [4]. The TOTP for CSD3 is separate from the University of Cambridge one, and the user needs to set up a new TOTP specifically for HPC [4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To resolve this issue, we need to reset the user's TOTP token and guide them through setting up a new TOTP for HPC. We will schedule a video call to verify the user's identity and then reset their TOTP token [2][3][6]. After the token is reset, the user can set up a new TOTP using the instructions provided on the HPC wiki [2][4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Are you available for a video call to verify your identity and reset your TOTP token?
- Have you tried setting up a new TOTP using the instructions on the HPC wiki [4]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To set up Microsoft Authenticator as your TOTP for HPC login, we need to reset your current TOTP token and guide you through setting up a new one. We will schedule a video call to verify your identity and then reset your token. Please let us know your availability for the call.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The TOTP for CSD3 is separate from the University of Cambridge one, and users need to set up a new TOTP specifically for HPC [4].
- We need to verify the user's identity before resetting their TOTP token [2][3][6].

user_input:
Hello,
I hope this email finds you well!
I am working on CSD3 for some cpu jobs, particularly nodes cclake and Icelake. I submitted some jobs last week and I found the queueing time very long. I am wondering if you can give advice on this regarding if there is anything I did wrong causing the long wait. Some general info:
- Name: Hongyu Zhou
- Account/PI: N-LAWRENCE-SL3-CPU
- Working directory: /home/hz488/project/ai_ranking_report
- Task:
- Data: I work with bibliographic data on hundreds of millions of paper including their title, abstract, and citation relationships
- Task:
- I am trying to calculate the cooccurence frequency of concepts in papers (two concepts used in one paper), for which the concepts are extracted already in our current data (one paper associated with a list of paper).
- I am building these cooccurrence networks for using papers since 1990 to current and group them to a interval of 3 years
- I generate networks for every three years.
- Problem:
- The queueing time is quite long.
- Slurm Job_id=10784619 Name=build_cooccur_net Began, Queued time 17:19:49
- I tried to minimise the potential RAM usage by using smaller intervals, using smaller samples and use sql-like ducked to calculate the concurrences. But in the future, there will definitely be larger jobs I need to run considering the size of my data in nature, especially if we were use Node2Vec to embed the networks we obtained.
- Job submission script:
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH -J build_cooccur_net
#SBATCH -A N-LAWRENCE-SL3-CPU
#SBATCH -p icelake
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=6
#SBATCH --mem=30G
#SBATCH --time=4:00:00
#SBATCH --output=1_build_net_duck_%j.out
#SBATCH --error=1_build_net_duck_%j.err
#SBATCH --mail-type=ALL
#SBATCH --mail-user=hz488@cam.ac.uk
set -euo pipefail
echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %T')] Job started"
module purge
module load python/3.8
source "$HOME/hzenv/bin/activate"
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
export MKL_NUM_THREADS=1
export OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS=1
TEMP_DIR="${SLURM_TMPDIR:-./temp_edges}"
mkdir -p "$TEMP_DIR"
python /home/hz488/project/ai_ranking_report/scripts/1_build_net_save_duckdb.py \
--input-dir /home/hz488/project/dimensions/publications/ \
--output-dir /home/hz488/project/data/ai_ranking_report/data/1_build_net \
--batch-size 200000 \
--batches-per-file 50 \
--sample-frac 1
echo "[$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %T')] Job finished”
Could you kindly advise me on this?
Best,
Hongyu

The user is experiencing long queue times for their jobs on the CSD3 cluster, specifically on the icelake partition [1][2]. The user's job submission script requests 1 node, 6 CPUs per task, and 30G of memory, with a time limit of 4 hours [1]. The queue time for the job is 17 hours and 19 minutes [1]. Similar issues have been reported by other users, with long queue times due to high utilization of the cluster [3][4][5].

ACTION
The user can try to optimize their job submission script to reduce the queue time. This can be done by requesting fewer resources, such as fewer nodes or CPUs, or by using a different partition [2][6]. The user can also try to submit their job at a less busy time, such as during the night or on weekends [7]. Additionally, the user can consider using the sapphire partition, which appears to be less congested [6].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about your job submission script and the resources you are requesting?
- Have you tried submitting your job to a different partition or at a less busy time?

Additionally, you can try submitting your job at a less busy time, such as during the night or on weekends. If you have any further questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to reach out to us.

Dear HPC team,
Good afternoon, I am contacting you since I am trying to run Freesurfer 8.0.0 on HPC but I am getting the following error:
FREESURFER: Undefined variable.
This makes me wonder if for some reason the path to freesurfer cannot be found. Since this is a new module can you please advise on the path where the application is so that I can use in my scripts? Has this been tested and was running as intended?
Needless to say I have loaded the module.
Best wishes,
Marialena

Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User loaded `freesurfer/8.0.0` and received `FREESURFER: Undefined variable`. From the original query, the most likely issue is incomplete environment setup after loading the module rather than the software being absent. The next step is to reproduce the module load, check the exact command or script the user is running, and then handle any further setup requirements such as the FreeSurfer setup script or licence configuration if they arise.

ACTION
1) Reproduce the issue and validate module behaviour.
2) Ask the user to show the exact commands or job script they are using.
3) Instruct the standard setup sequence:
   - `module load freesurfer/8.0.0`
   - `source $FREESURFER_HOME/SetUpFreeSurfer.sh`
4) If a subsequent error indicates a missing licence file, instruct the user to obtain a valid licence and set `FS_LICENSE=/path/to/license.txt`.
5) Confirm successful execution after the environment is set correctly.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hi Marialena,

Please first try the standard setup sequence:

module load freesurfer/8.0.0
source $FREESURFER_HOME/SetUpFreeSurfer.sh

Then retry the command that is failing.

If you then receive a separate licence-file error, please send that exact message and we can advise on setting `FS_LICENSE` correctly.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not request users place the licence file in protected system directories.
- Do not assume the initial undefined-variable error proves the software is missing or broken.

reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Login Node Health / Temporary Filesystem
Technical Action Required: Yes (diagnose node-specific issue)
Escalation Required: Optional (if persistent)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports compiler warning that `/tmp` is full and asks whether this relates to their own quota. `/tmp` is node-local temporary storage and can fill independently of the user’s `/home` or project quota.

ACTION
1) Explain what `/tmp` is:
   - temporary local filesystem on the node,
   - not the same as user/project quota shown by `quota`.
2) Ask user to report the node where error occurs (`hostname`).
3) Suggest immediate workaround: try compiling on another login node.
4) Escalate if issue persists across multiple nodes or for multiple users.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Loren,

The warning about `/tmp` means temporary space on the current node is full. This is separate from your `/home`/project quota, so your `quota` output can still look normal.

Please share the output of:
`hostname`

As a workaround, please try compiling on a different login node. If the issue appears on multiple nodes, let us know and we will escalate.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume user quota issue when warning explicitly references `/tmp`.

Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The error message "icc: warning #10352: The directory '/tmp' is full.  Please check disk space." indicates that the /tmp directory is full, which is preventing the compilation of the code [1]. This issue has been encountered before, and the solution has been to clear space in the /tmp directory [1][2][3][4][5][7].

ACTION
The support team should investigate which node is experiencing the full /tmp directory and clear space to resolve the issue [1][2][3][4][5][7].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Which node are you currently working on?
- Can you please run the command `df -h` and provide the output?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
The issue you are experiencing is due to the /tmp directory being full. We will investigate and clear space to resolve the issue. Please let us know which node you are currently working on.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The /tmp directory should be regularly cleaned up to prevent it from becoming full [1][2][3][4][5][7].
- Users should be aware of their disk usage and clean up unnecessary files to prevent quota exceeded errors [6].

Dear Sir/Madam,
My name is Guillaume Alexis, id : grfa2. My working directory is /rds/user/grfa2/hpc-work/
For now, I don't have a job submission script. I'm solely trying to install a package named Dedalus ([https://dedalus-project.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pages/installation.html](https://dedalus-project.readthedocs.io/en/latest/pages/installation.html)) and its dependancies, in order to run my script.
My main question is : How should I do it ? I tried in a conda environment, but I'm unsure about the intel/intel-mpi/fftw/hdf5 versions I should load, in order to build Dedalus correctly with these modules.
Thank you for your help,
Best wishes,
Guillaume Alexis
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Python Environment Management / Package Installation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user was trying to install Dedalus while relying on the cluster-wide conda module. The supported path is to install a personal Miniconda/Miniforge instance in `hpc-work`, then follow the upstream installation method from that personal environment. The user does not need to manually choose cluster MPI/FFTW module versions if they install the package through the documented conda-forge workflow.

ACTION
1) Do not use the shared cluster conda module for this workflow.
2) Install Miniconda or Miniforge in `~/rds/hpc-work`.
3) Create the environment there and follow the upstream Dedalus installation instructions.
4) Once installed, use Slurm or an interactive job as normal to run the code.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Guillaume,

Please avoid using the cluster’s shared conda module for this install. Instead, install your own Miniconda or Miniforge under `~/rds/hpc-work` and create the Dedalus environment there.

If you follow the Dedalus installation guidance from that personal environment, you should not need to manually select Intel MPI/FFTW module versions for the basic conda-forge install path.

Once the environment is ready, you can run the code via Slurm as usual, or use an interactive job if you want to test it first.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Prefer user-managed conda installs in `hpc-work` over the outdated shared conda module.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To install Dedalus, the user can follow the instructions provided in the ticket [1]. The user needs to load the required modules, including fftw, hdf5, and python, and then install Dedalus using pip. The user also needs to set environment variables, such as FFTW_PATH and MPI_PATH, to ensure that the package is installed correctly.

The user can use the following commands to install Dedalus:
```bash
module load fftw-3.3.6-pl2-intel-17.0.4-45kexpw
module load hdf5-1.10.1-intel-17.0.4-acprvzg
export FFTW_PATH=/usr/local/software/spack/spack-0.11.2/opt/spack/linux-rhel7-x86_64/intel-17.0.4/fftw-3.3.6-pl2-45kexpwgmgvqnuq4yse2vjee3hbvxnyb
export FFTW_LIBRARY_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/lib
export FFTW_INCLUDE_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/include
export MPI_PATH=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64
export MPI_LIBRARY_PATH=$MPI_PATH/lib
export MPI_INCLUDE_PATH=$MPI_PATH/include
pip install dedalus
```
The user can then activate the virtual environment and run Dedalus using the following commands:
```bash
source ~/venv-dedalus/bin/activate
LD_PRELOAD=$MPI_PATH/lib/libmpi.so python3 rayleigh_benard.py
```
To run Dedalus in parallel, the user can use the salloc command with the --ntasks-per-node option [1]. For example:
```bash
salloc -p skylake -N2 --ntasks-per-node=24 -t 1:0:0 --qos INTR
```
This will allocate 2 nodes with 24 tasks per node.

Dear Conor,
Please try to install additional software in your own account yourself. You can install dedalus by following the conda instructions at 
[https://dedalus-project.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html#alternative-installation-procedures](https://dedalus-project.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html#alternative-installation-procedures)

```
mkdir ~/rds/hpc-work/dedalus
cd ~/rds/hpc-work/dedalus
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DedalusProject/dedalus_conda/master/install_conda.sh
# Edit install_conda.sh:
BLAS="mkl"
PYTHON_VERSION="3.7"
# - end edits

module load miniconda/3
conda activate base
bash install_conda.sh
```
This will (at length) create a dedalus conda environment which you can then use by doing
conda activate dedalus
then running
python3 -m dedalus command
but please note the test, bench and cov functions don't work because of some problem in the python itself - some dependency issue not handled correctly by conda. If this method of installation doesn't in fact produce a functioning dedalus then please try one of the other installation methods described on the above web page, but please bear in mind that we can't support every item of 3rd party software that one can download from the internet.
In order to run this thing inside a job script then remember to perform the
module load miniconda/3
conda activate dedalus
commands in your job script.
Best regards,
Stuart

Christoforos Hadjigeorgiou commented:
Hi Louis,
For MPI and compilers those are already loaded as part of the default environment.
For HDF5 and FFTW you will also need to load and set some environment variables:
module load fftw-3.3.6-pl2-intel-17.0.4-45kexpw
module load hdf5-1.10.1-intel-17.0.4-acprvzg
export FFTW_PATH=/usr/local/software/spack/spack-0.11.2/opt/spack/linux-rhel7-x86_64/intel-17.0.4/fftw-3.3.6-pl2-45kexpwgmgvqnuq4yse2vjee3hbvxnyb
export FFTW_LIBRARY_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/lib
export FFTW_INCLUDE_PATH=$FFTW_PATH/include
export MPI_PATH=/usr/local/Cluster-Apps/intel/2017.4/compilers_and_libraries_2017.4.196/linux/mpi/intel64
export MPI_LIBRARY_PATH=$MPI_PATH/lib
export MPI_INCLUDE_PATH=$MPI_PATH/include
Some environment variables may be unnecessary but I set them to avoid any complains during install.
To install dedalus using pip in the virtual environment:
pip install dedalus
To install dedalus manually I ran while inside the venv:
hg clone [https://bitbucket.org/dedalus-project/dedaluscd](https://bitbucket.org/dedalus-project/dedaluscd) dedalus
pip install -r ./requirements
pip install .
1. These two steps may be unnecessary, you can try running dedalus without them first
 python3 ./setup.py build_ext --inplace 
 python3 ./setup.py install
 
To run dedalus for both pip and manual installation I did:
LD_PRELOAD=$MPI_PATH/lib/libmpi.so python3
Without the preload FFTW complains about not finding MPI functions.
Let us know how it goes.
Kind regards,
Chris

Jeffrey Salmond commented: Dear Vilda, I've just tried following some of the instructions on the Daedalus documentation, and I have found that the following script appears to produce a working version of Daedalus. It will take a while as it will build specific versions of fftw and hdf5 for you. If it works, then deadalus will be installed in your home directory in a virtualenv - use source ~/dedalus-install/bin/activate to enter the environment and use it. Hope this is helpful! Let us know how you get on... Best wishes, Jeffrey. #!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail module load python/3.6 export CC=mpiicc export CXX=mpiicpc export F77=mpiifort export F90=mpiifort export MPICC=$CC export MPICXX=$CXX VENV=$HOME/dedalus-install FFTW_BUILD=$(mktemp -d) HDF5_BUILD=$(mktemp -d) trap "rm -rf $FFTW_BUILD $HDF5_BUILD" EXIT virtualenv $VENV source $VENV/bin/activate cd $FFTW_BUILD FFTW='fftw-3.3.4' wget http://data.dedalus-project.org/dependencies/$FFTW.tar.gz tar xvf $FFTW.tar.gz cd $FFTW ./configure --prefix=$VENV --enable-shared --enable-mpi --enable-threads make && make install cd $HDF5_BUILD HDF5='hdf5-1.8.13' wget http://data.dedalus-project.org/dependencies/$HDF5.tar.gz tar xvf $HDF5.tar.gz cd $HDF5 ./configure --prefix=$VENV --enable-shared --enable-mpi --enable-threads make && make install export FFTW_PATH=$VENV export MPI_PATH=$I_MPI_ROOT export MPI_INCLUDE_PATH=$I_MPI_ROOT/include64 pip install dedalus View request: https://jira.hpc.cam.ac.uk/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/HPCSSUP-20287?sda_source=notification-email Turn off this request's notifications: https://jira.hpc.cam.ac.uk/servicedesk/customer/portal/2/HPCSSUP-20287/unsubscribe?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJxc2giOiJlYzRjMWRkMGEyNDM4M2UzZDk0ZDIzMDE4NmRlMDJhNzNjYmJlYWU5MmIyZjM0OTAzNWQ1ZDlkMTc0Y2ExMThjIiwiaXNzIjoic2VydmljZWRlc2stand0LXRva2VuLWlzc3VlciIsImNvbnRleHQiOnsidXNlciI6InZrbTI4QGNhbS5hYy51ayIsImlzc3VlIjoiSFBDU1NVUC0yMDI4NyJ9LCJleHAiOjE1NzIzNTk0OTMsImlhdCI6MTU2OTk0MDI5M30.PCqdX9HXKmAw5Nmekl4udcgtK8_Hso5Mk3S_GDaTd64 This is shared with Vilda Markeviciute ------------------------------ Help Center, powered by Jira Service Desk, sent you this message. http://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/service-desk?utm_medium=email&utm_source=service-desk_email-notification&utm_campaign=service-desk_email-notification

user_input:
Good afternoon,
A student from CEB would like to use abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster. The student mentioned that they contacted you and you advised that this version is not compatible.
I wanted to confirm if this is the case or did the end user misunderstand.
Kind regards,
Zac
------------------------------------------------------
Zac Iskander (he/him)
IT Support Team Leader
University of Cambridge
Department of Engineering
Trumpington Street
Cambridge, CB2 1PZ, UK
01223760591
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Version Support / Licensing
Technical Action Required: No (policy clarification)
Escalation Required: Yes (new build request if required)
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The immediate answer is that `abaqus/2024` is not currently available as a centrally supported version on the cluster; the latest supported central version is `abaqus/2022`. If there is a requirement for a newer version, licensing and a separate build or install request would need to be coordinated.

ACTION
1) Confirm that `abaqus/2022` is the latest supported central version.
2) Explain that no timeline can be promised for `abaqus/2024`.
3) If the relevant licence owner wants a newer centrally available version, ask them to submit a fresh build or install request to HPC support.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Zac,

At present the newest centrally supported version on the cluster is `abaqus/2022`, so the user did not misunderstand.

If the relevant licence owner wants a newer centrally available version, please send a new request to `support@hpc.cam.ac.uk` and we can look into building and providing that version.

Best wishes,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Abaqus [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The student from CEB wants to use Abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster, but it is unclear if this version is compatible with the cluster [1]. The HPC support team has previously stated that they do not have Abaqus 2024 as a module or license on CSD3 [4].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To confirm compatibility, please check the available Abaqus modules on the cluster using the command `module av abaqus` [3]. If Abaqus 2024 is not available, the student may need to use a different version or install it themselves [4].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the exact error message the student receives when trying to use Abaqus 2024 on the cluster?
- Has the student tried using a different version of Abaqus on the cluster?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your inquiry about using Abaqus 2024 on the HPC cluster. Unfortunately, we do not have this version as a module or license on CSD3. You may need to use a different version or install it yourself. Please try checking the available modules using `module av abaqus` and let us know if you have any further questions.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The HPC support team does not provide support for licensed products like Abaqus [1].
- Users should check the available modules and licenses before attempting to use software on the cluster [3].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-91919
[3] : HPCSSUP-75720
[4] : HPCSSUP-81168

Hi Team,
I’m getting in touch regarding an issue on the SRCP platform, which I’ve attached for reference. We urgently need the SNPolisher R package installed into the environment associated with the following platform ID: kjtcs2jvzw7.
This is time‑critical, as the NHSBT Genomics Programme is currently unable to carry out essential clinical work without this package being available.
Could you please prioritise this installation at your earliest opportunity and confirm once it has been completed?
Grateful
[cid:b9547aa4-5bed-4828-8cca-804065ce11a4]

user_input:
Hi
I've been getting an error on Ampere jobs recently where it fails immediately, stating that the CUDA-capable devices aren't available. I've had this error occur when using submission scripts for running turbostream 3.6.10 which has worked for years, and using a separate script running turbostream 4.2.127 recently. I've attached an example slurm.out file I've had from a turbostream4 job. This error seems to happen whenever I get assigned gpu-q-29, though I've heard colleagues mention getting something similar recently on different nodes. I'm aware I can exclude gpu-q-29 in my sbatch, but if it happens on other nodes and I lose all the time I waited in the queue, it's frustrating. Are there any sbatch instructions I can add to avoid this happening, or a fix for the route problem?
Many Thanks,
Dom

CLASSIFICATION
Category: GPU Runtime Failure / Node Health / Slurm Configuration
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The job is intermittently failing on a specific GPU node with CUDA device availability errors. Two grounded mitigations from the submitted command/output are to ensure the correct Ampere base module stack is loaded and to let Open MPI infer task placement from Slurm instead of forcing `-npernode` and `-np`.

ACTION
1) Load the correct base stack:
   `module purge`
   `module load rhel8/default-amp`
2) Remove manual `mpirun -npernode ... -np ...` overrides where possible.
3) Prefer `--tasks-per-node=4` rather than only `--ntasks=8` for the reported two-node case.
4) As a temporary workaround, route around the bad node with:
   `#SBATCH --exclude=gpu-q-29`
5) If the issue persists after these changes or starts affecting other nodes, escalate for node-health investigation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Dom,

Please first make sure your script loads `rhel8/default-amp`, as that adds the expected CUDA runtime environment.

We also recommend removing the explicit `-npernode`/`-np` arguments and letting Open MPI infer task placement from Slurm. For a two-node, eight-task job, `--tasks-per-node=4` is the safer Slurm-side request.

In the short term, you can avoid the problematic node with:
`#SBATCH --exclude=gpu-q-29`

Best wishes,
HPC Support

user_input:
Hi there,
I manage a Research Data store for the “kstarkstar” project:
Directory: rds-mk652-kstarkstar
Project ID: bUoO2gzww9Q
It is about to expire. I want to extend the license for another year (for all 5TB) with all of the same permissions. I raised a PO have requested a new data store but think I may have made a mistake here. I don’t want a new storage area, I just want to extend the existing one.
Please can you resolve my mistake?
The PO for the extension is attached.
Many thanks,
Matt
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Renewal / Procurement Correction
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user wants to extend an existing storage project but believes they may have accidentally requested a new storage purchase instead. The safe handling is to stop them from activating anything new until support has verified which service record should be extended and whether the mistaken request can be corrected or needs to be cancelled or reissued.

ACTION
1) Ask the user not to activate the mistaken newly purchased storage project.
2) Confirm which existing project is the one that should be extended.
3) Check internally whether the mistaken request can be removed or converted.
4) If cancellation or reissue is required, direct the user to the appropriate purchasing or finance route.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hi Matt,

Please leave the accidentally purchased storage project unallocated while we verify whether the mistaken request can be corrected or whether it needs to be cancelled and reissued.

Once we confirm the correct path, we will tell you whether any purchasing or finance action is needed on your side.

Best wishes,
HPC Support
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Licence Management [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: Yes [1]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user wants to extend the license for their Research Data Store (RDS) for another year with the same permissions, but they accidentally raised a purchase order (PO) for a new data store instead of extending the existing one [1]. The user has a directory named rds-mk652-kstarkstar with a project ID of bUoO2gzww9Q and wants to extend the license for 5TB [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Cancel the new data store request and free up the PO [4].
2. Use the PO to extend the existing RDS project (rds-mk652-kstarkstar) for another year with the same permissions [1].
3. Ensure that the extension is done correctly to avoid any issues with the license [7].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please confirm the exact details of the PO that was raised for the new data store [1]?
- Are there any specific requirements or constraints for extending the license for the existing RDS project [1]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We will cancel the new data store request and use the PO to extend the existing RDS project (rds-mk652-kstarkstar) for another year with the same permissions. Please let us know if there are any further issues or concerns.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the extension is done correctly to avoid any issues with the license [7].
- Only authorized personnel should be able to extend or modify RDS licenses [1].

[1] : HPCSSUP-20650
[4] : HPCSSUP-41637
[7] : HPCSSUP-61074

We can ask portal developers to cancel newly created licence but PO needs to be deleted by office who created it so most likely your local finance office. Greg
RCS Team
